title: Sort all CLI flags in podman commands
body:
This commit fixes #1610  in podman build
Signed-off-by: Qi Wang <qiwan@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Errors in the test don't appear to be related to this change.  

```
mount: /sys/kernel/security: cannot mount none read-only.
Could not mount /sys/kernel/security.
AppArmor detection and --privileged mode might break.
mount: /tmp: cannot mount none read-only.
```

@TomSweeneyRedHat tests are currently broken everywhere, but we are on it in https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/1617.

@QiWang19 thanks for the nice fix!
It can be fixed by hand. Do I need to use the sort function ```sortFlags```?

@QiWang19 I'm not sure which can be fixed by hand?  I'd vote to use it for all, even if they could be fixed by hand, but at the very least by any of the commands that pull in more than one set of flags like build does.  If we use it everywhere, we won't have to worry about someone making a bad change at some point in time in the future.

> [...] If we use it everywhere, we won't have to worry about someone making a bad change at some point in time in the future.

I agree with @TomSweeneyRedHat. @QiWang19, could you sort the flags of all commands unconditionally? This way, we avoid potential regressions in the future, for instance, when some flag gets added.
@TomSweeneyRedHat @vrothberg Sure, I've sort the flags of all commands unconditionally.
@QiWang19 Mind rebasing to pick up CI fixes?
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably #1607) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
@QiWang19 Sorry, mind rebasing one more time? Should be good to merge once the conflict is fixed
